composition.Programmer 1:
Implement backend code in app.py
- Develop the main backend Python application file.
- The website should be accessible via local port 5000, with the first page accessible at route '/' (Dashboard page).
- Implement routing for all nine frontend pages, ensuring each route corresponds to the frontend code.
- Handle reading and writing to local text files in the 'data' directory using plain text, starting from the first line, without encryption.
- Support all functionalities  browsing articles by category, reading detailed articles, bookmarking favorites, viewing comments, writing comments, tracking trending articles, searching articles, and navigation.
- Frontend code files to be supported  dashboard.html, article_catalog.html, article_details.html, bookmarks.html, comments.html, write_comment.html, trending.html, category.html, search_results.html.
- Incorporate the original task's supplementary condition  no authentication required; all features directly accessible.
composition.Programmer 2:
Develop frontend templates in the 'templates/' directory
- Complete all nine HTML template files  dashboard.html, article_catalog.html, article_details.html, bookmarks.html, comments.html, write_comment.html, trending.html, category.html, search_results.html.
- Each template must include the specified elements with their IDs as per the requirements document.
- All URLs in the frontend code should be routes defined in the backend, not direct HTML file names.
- Ensure the UI supports the functionalities  featured articles, article browsing, detailed article view, bookmarks management, comments viewing and writing, trending articles display, category filtering, and search results display.
composition.Programmer 3:
Develop static assets in the 'static/' directory
- Implement CSS styles in css/styles.css for consistent styling across all pages.
- Implement JavaScript in js/scripts.js for client-side interactivity, including button actions and dynamic content updates.
- Optionally add images (thumbnails or icons) in static/images/ to enhance UI.
- Ensure static assets support the frontend templates and improve user experience.
